# The Subtle Art of Exaptation

Subtitle: `How Almost Nothing New Ever Works, and Why That’s a Relief`

## Description / Excerpt

An essay on exaptation, reuse, religion, artifacts, ambiguity, and why sustainable change often redirects existing substrates rather than inventing from scratch.

Excerpt:

Or: how almost nothing new ever works, and why that’s a relief Human beings are terrible at inventing things from scratch. This is not a moral failing. It’s a structural one. When we try to invent something wholly new—new values, new systems, new ways of coordinating—we almost al
